title: Session 23 Meta-Analysis (Waves 141-149)
description: 150th wave milestone. Integration of 9 analysis waves from session 23.

session_findings.title: System Organ Class Sex Bias Taxonomy
session_findings.key_result: 16 SOC categories classified. Hematological most female (70.0%F), Ocular most male (36.0%F).

session_findings.title: Temporal Sex Bias Dynamics
session_findings.key_result: 30.4pp volume gradient: Q1 42.9%F to Q5 73.3%F. Volume-direction rho=0.2065.

session_findings.title: Multi-SOC Drug Sex Bias Profiles
session_findings.key_result: 915 drugs span 5+ SOC categories. Etanercept: 100pp cross-SOC range. Consistency drops with breadth.

session_findings.title: Report Count Threshold Sensitivity
session_findings.key_result: Female bias peaks at t=300 (68.6%F), regresses at t=5000 (54.2%F). 92/544 drugs flip direction.

session_findings.title: Adverse Event Sex Specificity Index
session_findings.key_result: 473 strongly female AEs vs 389 strongly male. Haemarthrosis 100%F, Abortion spontaneous 0%F (paradoxical).

session_findings.title: Drug-AE Concordance Analysis
session_findings.key_result: Mean AE concordance 64.5% (>50% random). 341 AEs >=90% concordant. Concordance increases with strength.

session_findings.title: Extreme Signal Characterization
session_findings.key_result: TESTOSTERONE+Prostatic disorder: logR=8.87 (most extreme). Male reversal at |logR|>=6: 90% male. Male extremes stronger (3.624 vs 3.489).

session_findings.title: Drug Class Pharmacological Sex Bias
session_findings.key_result: ACE inhibitors 72.1%F, SSRIs paradoxically 44.3%F. Opioids 70.9%F. mAbs span 17-73%F range.

session_findings.title: Shannon Entropy of Sex-Differential Profiles
session_findings.key_result: 41 drugs with H=0 (perfectly predictable), 190 with H>0.99 (near-random). Mean H=0.7977.

novel_discoveries: 1. MALE REVERSAL AT EXTREMES: |logR|>=5 is 78.6% male, |logR|>=6 is 90% male — overturning the default female-predominance narrative
2. FEMALE BIAS PEAKS AND REGRESSES: Maximum at t=300 (68.6%F), falls to 54.2%F at t=5000 — partial regression to the mean
3. CROSS-SOC RANGE: Same drug can be 0%F in one organ system and 100%F in another (etanercept)
4. 64.5% AE CONCORDANCE: Sex-bias direction is partly AE-intrinsic, not purely drug-dependent
5. SSRI PARADOX: Antidepressants are 44.3%F despite 2:1 female depression prevalence
6. 41 ZERO-ENTROPY DRUGS: Perfectly predictable sex-bias across all adverse events
7. TESTOSTERONE+PROSTATIC DISORDER: logR=8.87 is the most extreme signal in 96,281
8. STRENGTH GRADIENT INTENSIFIES with higher report thresholds (4.8pp at t=10 → 13.1pp at t=500)
key_findings: Male reversal at distributional extremes is the most novel finding
AE concordance > 50% proves biological component to sex-differential signals
Drug class analysis reveals pharmacological basis for sex bias patterns
